reasoning: The probability of a new vaccine being developed for leprosy and added to the Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' by the resolution dates in 2025 is very low. Currently, there are no fully approved leprosy-specific vaccines (the most prominent candidate, LepVax, is only entering Phase 1b/2 clinical trials in early 2025). As of April 2025, the Wikipedia article has a blank entry for the Leprosy vaccine, which determines a 'No' resolution. The likelihood of an approved vaccine emerging within the next 1-6 months is essentially zero, so a 'Yes' resolution would depend on someone successfully editing the Wikipedia page to include an existing cross-protective vaccine (like BCG or MIP) or mistakenly listing LepVax as a fully created vaccine, and the edit surviving until the resolution date. The chances of this happening and persisting on the specific resolution dates are very small, justifying initial probabilities of 2%, slightly increasing up to 4% for the most distant date due to general uncertainty and the possibility of random Wikipedia edits.
